# Math Family — Site-Wide Design Research (2026-06-13)

*Generated by a 48-agent research swarm: 19 page/system audits + 6 benchmark researchers + 20 personas + 20 journey-walks + Opus synthesis + critic. Raw run: wf_ed92f7ac-cf2.*

**Counts:** {"audits":19,"benchmarks":6,"personas":20,"journeys":20}

## Audit scorecard

| Page | UX | Visual | A11y | SEO | Conv |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| parkmath — / (home, nav hub redesign) | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 3 |

## Cross-cutting themes

- **[high]** Answer-first promise breaks below the fold: static SSR hero numbers and live calculator state diverge
  - _Parking hub, duration, lounge, and roaming country pages render AnswerLead/SavesVerdict/chart from a hardcoded SSR default (7-day / 3 visits / 7d-5GB) while the calculator updates client-side. Priya (14d), Mei-Ling (14d), Kwame (21d), Tom (lounge slider) all hit the contradiction: the big confident headline disagrees with the tool. SavesVerdict on lounge/roaming is decoupled from slider state._
- **[high]** The fold is buried under scaffolding and editorial before the answer/tool appears
  - _Dave (130% font) and Fatima see H1 + FreshnessBadge + SourceCitation + CaveatChips before any number. Priya scrolls 3–4 screen-heights of AnswerLead+SavesVerdict+SVG chart before the ParkingCalculator. ParkMath home AirportSearch shows ~30 airport cards before StatStrip/tiles. Benchmark: 44–55% of AI citations + LCP come from the first 30%/300px._
- **[high]** Touch targets and tap-the-whole-row are systematically below 44px
  - _SegmentedControl min-h-9 (36px); duration nav, network nav lists, baggage/lounge cross-links, SourceCitation, inline 'More at this airport' lists all ~18–24px. FeeGrid mobile card rows with a Link in cells[0] aren't tappable as a unit (roaming + baggage indexes). WCAG 2.5.8/2.5.5; EAA in force June 2025._
- **[medium]** Per-column type treatment is wrong: prose/boolean cells render as right-aligned tabular figures
  - _FeeGrid defaults numeric to j>0, so Notes (baggage), Yes/No Priority Pass (lounges), and lounge counts render mf-num right-aligned, and 'Free' looks identical to '£45'. Multiple pages omit numericColumns; the lounge index spends a column on a count that should be Priority Pass coverage._
- **[high]** Conditional/empty data states are silent — pages look broken or, worse, mislead
  - _Free airports (Birmingham/Inverness/Newcastle) suppress all penalty/caveat UI, hiding the 10-min/no-return gotcha (Fatima, Ananya). priorYearFeePence null = trendNote vanishes with no note (Olusegun). Gatwick/Birmingham gate-only → SavesVerdict filler + a misleading £0-saving chart (James, Nigel). Missing destinations (Taiwan/Nigeria/Liverpool) 404 with no fallback (Mei-Ling, Kwame, Claire)._
- **[high]** Affiliate cards float free of a verified number, inverting trust hierarchy
  - _Affiliate blocks use raw bg-blue-50 outside the token system, show 'up to 25% off' with no denominator (James, Priya), and on mobile render a stripped link with no USPs. Booking-options 'official site' ghost vs filled affiliate inverts hierarchy; the Airalo slot is inactive so Kwame's core task is unreachable. Desktop disclosure sits after the CTA (ASA/CAP risk)._
- **[high]** Hardcoded/generic copy drifts from the verified data beside it
  - _CaveatChip 'Pay before you leave' contradicts actual midnight-next-day deadlines (Heathrow/Manchester/Newcastle — Gerald, Rosa, Helen). AnswerCard 'for up to 1 min' on per-entry tariffs (Helen). Hardcoded year '2026' in parking titles. Free-airport meta descriptions promise penalty/deadline that don't exist (Richard). 'barrier-free' mistranslates to 'accessible' for non-native readers (Rosa, Brigitte)._
- **[medium]** Freshness is shown as a date but not as confidence — and the aggregate date can mislead
  - _FreshnessBadge shows the newest verifiedAt across a dataset, so index pages can read 'verified yesterday' while rows are weeks stale (Marcus). verifiedAt is collected but never surfaced on news cards/detail. No 'unchanged since last check' delta (Olusegun, Claire, James). ISO dates shown raw in some places, en-GB elsewhere. Benchmark: pages lose AI citations 3x faster without visible cadence._
- **[high]** Accessibility primitives missing across shared components used on every page
  - _FaqAccordion uses <details>/<summary> + a ⌄ glyph, no aria-expanded — iOS VoiceOver regression (Gerald, Stuart). No aria-live on geolocation/calculator result announcements. News card nests <article> in <a>. Before→after price arrow aria-hidden with no spoken transition. Color-only positive/winner cues fail deuteranopia (Zeynep). EmailCaptureSlot/SegmentedControl lack focus-visible rings._
- **[high]** Comparison tables and answers are not maximally extractable for GEO/AI citation
  - _Index pages miss BreadcrumbList/FAQPage/Dataset/Product+Offer schema that detail pages already have (airport-parking, lounges, baggage, price-index). No SpeakableSpecification. Summaries bury the peak figure. Drop-off lacks 'kiss and fly'/'set-down' synonyms. Benchmark: tables 4.2x citation rate, answer-first 3.1x, Dataset schema 4.3–6.1x._
- **[medium]** Shared system has structural gaps that will compound: no dark layer, thin icons, no Safari fences, no density tokens, desktop-only maps
  - _tokens.css is a single light @theme with no semantic-alias/dark split. line-glyphs exports only RunwayDivider vs the ~8 semantic icons the 'colour is never the only signal' rule needs. @property --mf-angle conic shine has no @supports fence (Safari artefact). No spacing/density tokens. StatStrip hardcodes grid-cols-3. UkMap/RegionMap are hidden sm:block with no mobile IATA+city fallback for 96% of traffic; Google-Translate breaks FeeGrid CSS-grid cells on budget Android (Rosa)._
- **[medium]** No persistent answer/share mechanism for users who came to relay one fact
  - _Nearly every journey wants to copy/share/screenshot a single fact (Priya, Dave, Ananya, Rosa, Fatima, Tom, Helen, Marcus). No copy-to-WhatsApp, no Web Share, no URL-encoded calculator state (?days=14&data=5), no print stylesheet (Claire, Gerald). MiniAnswerBar exists but is non-focusable and not recoverable by keyboard (Stuart)._

## Design directions

### The Verified Instrument (evolve the trust DNA)

Keep the calm fintech register and double down on the one thing competitors can't fake: every number is live, sourced, dated, and shareable.

- **Principles:** Answer-first is literal: the verified figure is the LCP element, above all chrome, on every page; One reactive source of truth per page — SSR hero and live calculator are never allowed to disagree; Freshness is confidence, not a timestamp: deltas ('unchanged since last check'), oldest-row honesty, human-voice verification; Transparency is the brand: full cost as typed line items (Wise), affiliate price beside the verified one, disclosure before the CTA; Restraint — neutral-dominant surfaces, the single brand-accent glow reserved for the one recommendation per screen; Built for relay: copy/share/print every answer (the dominant real-world job across journeys)
- **Signature moves:** Reactive answer architecture: lift SSR defaults into URL-param-driven state (?days=14) so AnswerLead/SavesVerdict/chart/MiniAnswerBar always reflect inputs; 'Verified' chip evolves into a confidence pill: source label, en-GB date, and a YoY/since-last-check delta; aggregate badges show the oldest row; Copy-for-WhatsApp + Web Share + print stylesheet on every answer card; URL-encoded calculator state for bookmark/share; Tabular lining figures (font-variant-numeric, true minus, trailing zeros) wherever a number can update; bind hero results to --text-stat/display; System-wide accessibility pass: live regions, ARIA-disclosure FaqAccordion, 44px targets, focus-visible rings; Honest empty states: gate-only, free-airport, missing-destination, null-prior-year all render an explicit sourced sentence instead of silence
- **Trade-offs:** Lowest design risk and highest trust payoff, but visually evolutionary — it will not feel like a dramatic relaunch, and the win depends on disciplined data-pipeline + state plumbing rather than surface polish. Underwhelms if judged purely on screenshots.
- **Best for:** The safest, highest-ROI bet given the audits: it directly fixes the SSR/calculator contradiction, freshness honesty, touch targets, accessibility, and the share/relay gap that recur across all 20 journeys, while preserving the navy/teal calm-instrument identity already shipped.
- **References:** Wise (pre-commitment fee transparency), Monzo (calm summary-first fold), Which? / MoneySavingExpert (date-stamped verified figures), NerdWallet (answer-first calculator + fact-checked byline)

### Open Data Pressroom (bold editorial data-brand)

Reframe the family as a citable UK travel-cost data publisher — ONS/FT-Data aesthetic, tables and charts engineered to be quoted by journalists and AI engines.

- **Principles:** The dataset is the product: structured tables and movers leaderboards are the hero, not a calculator afterthought; Every page is a primary source — visible methodology, sources, CSV export, and a one-click citation; Editorial confidence in type: fluid display headings, tabular figures, generous data-ink, charts paired with accessible HTML tables; Movement is the story: lead with the peak figure, the biggest mover, the year-on-year change; Maximally extractable: Dataset/Product/Offer/Speakable schema, answer-first 40–75 word passages, question-form H2s; Authority signals: named compiler/byline, entity graph (Organization/Person sameAs), consistent freshness cadence
- **Signature moves:** Promote movers/index data to a card-strip or leaderboard with up/down semantic icons and bold mono deltas (FT-Data/ONS register); Embed the SVG charts with descriptive alt + an accessible HTML-table twin; OG/Speakable on the most-cited URLs; 'Cite this table' affordance (pre-formatted citation + Dataset URL + verified range) and prominent CSV 'Download the data' bands; Full schema sweep across index pages (BreadcrumbList/FAQPage/Dataset/Product+Offer), synonym-rich meta, lead-with-the-peak summaries; A frozen-left-column horizontal-scroll comparison table component (sticky first col, right-edge fade) for 3+ option pages; Methodology + compiler byline + entity-graph schema to manufacture the third-party citation signal for a new domain
- **Trade-offs:** Strongest for SEO/GEO/press and differentiation, but pushes the interactive calculator into a secondary role — risks under-serving the impatient mobile relay user (Dave, Fatima, Ananya) who wants one number, not a dataset. Heavier editorial/ops cadence to sustain freshness credibly.
- **Best for:** Maximising AI-citation share, journalist backlinks, and the open-data link-magnet strategy already in flight (price-index, /data CSVs, ai-citation-check skill). Best if growth is bet on earned media + GEO rather than on-page conversion.
- **References:** FT Data / FT Visual & Data Journalism, ONS data dashboards, The Economist / Our World in Data, StockBrokers.com (disclosed-methodology rigor)

### Premium Depth (glass/elevation, restrained)

A tactile, layered instrument — soft elevation, a single rotating accent edge on the recommendation, microstate-complete interactions — that feels crafted without compromising data legibility.

- **Principles:** Depth signals importance: layered shadows and one accent-lit edge mark the single recommended/cheapest element per screen; Microstate completeness: all six states (default/hover/focus/active/disabled/loading) custom-designed on every control; Functional motion only: staggered card entry, count-up to the verified saving, pressed-state CTAs — all gated behind motion-safe; Glass is a frame, never the data: depth lives on cards and the winner row; numbers stay on flat high-contrast surfaces; Skeletons that match layout, not spinners, for any client-fetched result; Progressive enhancement with graceful fallbacks (Safari @supports fences, reduced-motion, no-JS static answer)
- **Signature moves:** Reserve mf-edge-shine / mf-glow-winner for the one winner element and make it persistent across breakpoints (mobile card + desktop rest state); Count-up AnimatedNumber from gate price to verified saving on first paint (motion-safe), with a static reduced-motion value; Layout-matched skeleton screens for calculator results; pressed/scale CTA states and staggered fade-up for result lists; Build out the six-state microstate system in tokens (custom focus-visible rings, loading geometry) as the polish layer; @supports-fenced conic shine with a static hairline fallback; semantic-alias token split enabling a future dark depth theme; Mobile depth fallbacks: an IATA+city elevation pill where UkMap/RegionMap are hidden, so the void becomes a crafted anchor
- **Trade-offs:** Highest perceived-quality ceiling and the most 'relaunch' feel, but the riskiest for a trust-first/YMYL audience and the most expensive to do right — depth/motion can read as gimmick or harm legibility/contrast (Zeynep), and demand rigorous accessibility/perf discipline (CLS, prefers-reduced-motion, Safari). Does not by itself fix the data/state contradictions.
- **Best for:** Layering on top of the Verified Instrument once correctness is fixed — a differentiation/polish pass for the home hubs and winner moments. Weakest as a standalone first move because it adds surface before fixing the substance the journeys complain about.
- **References:** Linear (microstate craft, restrained depth), Stripe (quiet financial-data polish), Revolut (premium tactile fintech), Vercel dashboard (elevation + motion discipline)

## Roadmap

### P0 — Design-system foundation (shared, both brands)
- **Scope:** Split tokens.css into brand-primitive + semantic-alias layers behind a dark/[data-theme] block (no visual change). Ship the system-wide a11y + touch-target pass: SegmentedControl and all nav/cross-links to 44px, custom focus-visible rings, ARIA-disclosure FaqAccordion, live-region/role=alert helper, @supports fence on mf-edge-shine. Add ~5–8 semantic stroke icons to line-glyphs. Add density/cell tokens; FeeGrid gains rowHref/highlightRow/highlightColumn/correct numericColumns defaults and a persistent cross-breakpoint winner; StatStrip dynamic columns; GlintController no-op guard.
- **Why:** Every page consumes packages/ui, so these are the highest-leverage, lowest-risk fixes and they unblock all later passes. Touch targets, FAQ a11y, per-column typing and the winner-row treatment are cited on nearly every audited page; EAA/WCAG 2.2 make them non-optional.

### P1 — Truth & freshness correctness (data + copy)
- **Scope:** Kill hardcoded copy that drifts from data: CaveatChip uses real paymentDeadline; AnswerCard per-entry label; conditional meta descriptions; drop 'barrier-free'/'Click' wording. Honest empty states for free-airport gotchas, gate-only parking (guard the £0 chart), null prior-year, and missing destinations (graceful 404 with nearest alternatives). FreshnessBadge shows oldest-row/delta and en-GB dates everywhere. Surface verifiedAt on news cards/detail.
- **Why:** These are trust-destroying correctness bugs (Gerald, Rosa, Helen, Nigel, Olusegun, Fatima) that no visual polish offsets, and they are mostly low-effort string/branch changes against data the records already carry.

### P2 — Reactive answer-first fold (calculator pages)
- **Scope:** Unify SSR hero and live calculator into one URL-param-driven source of truth across parking hub/duration, lounge, and roaming country/network pages (AnswerLead/SavesVerdict/chart/MiniAnswerBar all reflect inputs). Reorder folds so the verified hero number is the LCP element above chrome. Bind results to --text-stat/display + tabular figures. Lift SavesVerdict into calculators; add break-even line; raise lounge slider max + numeric input.
- **Why:** The SSR/live contradiction is the single most-repeated high-severity journey friction (Priya, Mei-Ling, Kwame, Tom, Nigel) and directly breaks the answer-first/verified DNA that defines both brands.

### P3 — Conversion & affiliate integrity
- **Scope:** Token-swap affiliate cards into the system (bg-brand-accent/[0.06] + verify eyebrow), fix CTA hierarchy (affiliate primary, official-site secondary), unify mobile/desktop HolidayExtras layout, move disclosure before the CTA, show a concrete affiliate price/saving beside the verified figure, activate the Airalo slot. Promote BookingOptions below the calculator + a winner-row 'book this' link. Add copy/share/print + URL-encoded state.
- **Why:** Conversion scored lowest across the audit (often 2/5); affiliate blocks currently float free of verified numbers and invert trust hierarchy. Fixing this monetises the now-correct, now-reactive pages and serves the relay/share job every persona wanted.

### P4 — GEO / schema / editorial extractability
- **Scope:** Schema sweep on index pages (BreadcrumbList/FAQPage/Dataset/Product+Offer/Speakable); enrich datasetLd; answer-first 40–75 word passages with question-form H2s; lead summaries with the peak figure; synonym coverage; embed price-index charts with accessible HTML-table twins + OG images; 'Cite this table' + CSV bands; compiler byline + Organization/Person entity graph.
- **Why:** GEO/AI-citation is a parallel traffic channel central to the strategy (ai-citation-check, /data magnets) and benefits from now-clean tables and answers. Detail pages already have partial schema; index pages are the gap. Sequenced after correctness so cited data is accurate.

### P5 — Premium depth & motion polish
- **Scope:** Layer the elevation/winner-edge/count-up/skeleton/microstate system onto home hubs and winner moments, motion-safe and Safari-fenced, plus mobile depth fallbacks (IATA+city pill) and the dark-mode theme enabled by the P0 token split. Add color-blind-safe winner/positive cues (icon+label, not hue alone).
- **Why:** Highest perceived-quality lift but riskiest for a YMYL audience, so it comes last — applied only after substance (correctness, reactivity, conversion, GEO) is solid, and gated by accessibility/perf so depth never harms legibility.

## Critic — gaps & risks

**Verdict:** The synthesis is comprehensive and mostly correct but has three structural weaknesses that undermine its roadmap: P2 and P3 are artificially decoupled when they share a single root fix; direction 2 (Open Data Pressroom) conflates a journalist-citation product with a consumer-dashboard product without resolving which wins; and the entire document is silent on performance budgets despite recommending changes (hero-number promotion, calculator hydration, affiliate card re-render) that are the highest CLS/INP risk moves in the stack — shipping P2 without a performance gate could trade SEO ranking for the very answer-first visibility the recommendations are trying to achieve.

- P2 ('Reactive answer-first fold') and P3 ('Conversion & affiliate integrity') are sequenced as distinct phases but share one root cause: the calculator result is never the authoritative source for the affiliate CTA. You cannot fix the affiliate trust hierarchy (P3) until the calculator output is the single hero number (P2) — these are one coupled problem, not two phases. Splitting them risks shipping corrected-but-disconnected affiliate cards in P3 that still reference stale SSR defaults because P2 RSC refactor isn't done yet.
- The roadmap has no performance phase and no measurable performance budget. CLS risk from the SSR→client calculator hydration swap (P2), INP risk from FeeGrid re-renders on rowHref/slider changes (P3), and LCP risk from promoting the hero number above the fold (P2) are all architectural consequences of the top recommendations — but no phase addresses Next.js streaming, Suspense boundaries, skeleton shapes, or a Core Web Vitals acceptance threshold. This is an unacknowledged delivery risk, not a polish item.
- The synthesis treats 'direction 3: Premium Depth' as standalone-viable with the caveat 'riskiest for YMYL.' The deeper problem is that mf-edge-shine, count-up AnimatedNumber, and skeleton screens each produce measurable CLS/FID/INP regressions on budget Android (the Rosa persona already cited Google Translate + CSS-grid breakage on budget Android). Direction 3's signature moves can actively break the Core Web Vitals that power organic ranking — this tension is unresolved, not just noted.
- No persona represents a repeat/return visitor or an alert subscriber. Every audited journey is first-visit / single-task. The email capture slot, news hub, and freshness cadence are all built around retention mechanics (alerts, RSS, pricing-change news) but no journey tests whether those mechanics actually close the re-engagement loop. The synthesis therefore has zero evidence for or against the email/news retention channel working, yet it recommends it in P4 and on nearly every per-page list.
- The 'Open Data Pressroom' direction (direction 2) is presented as clean, but it silently contains two incompatible product bets: (a) a journalist/AI-citation play (Dataset schema, CSV download, cite-this-table) and (b) a consumer data dashboard (movers leaderboards, YoY card strips). These require different page densities, reading-level registers, and update cadences. Conflating them as one direction means neither is executed with conviction — the synthesis should either split them or declare which audience is primary.
- The accessibility theme (#9 in the synthesis) correctly lists FaqAccordion, aria-live, focus-visible, color-only cues — but misses a structural gap: both apps have a single error.tsx boundary (parkmath only; roammath has none in the graph) with no loading.tsx or Suspense boundaries visible in the route tree. A screen-reader user navigating between pages that suspend with no loading boundary gets silence, then a full page paint — no progressive announcement. This is an a11y-architecture omission, not just a component-level fix.
- The synthesis identifies the Airalo slot as 'inactive' (Kwame's core task is unreachable) but treats it as a P3 conversion fix. It is actually a P1 trust-and-correctness bug: a roaming country page that surfaces an eSIM CTA linking to a dead/inactive affiliate presents the verified-instrument brand promise as broken. Activating Airalo is a data/partnership task with a lead time that the roadmap does not account for; if it slips, the synthesis has no fallback (e.g., suppress the slot, show a 'coming soon' with a direct Airalo link, or substitute Holafly/Saily).
- The synthesis never addresses the cross-brand linking and shared-session problem. Priya planning a 14-day trip wants parking AND roaming data, but ParkMath and RoamMath are separate domains with no shared navigation, no cross-brand search, and no URL that surfaces both answers together. The 'MathFamily' umbrella exists in the codebase (packages/ui, packages/engine, FamilyLinks component) but no journey audited, and no direction proposed, addresses the combined 'going abroad by car' user whose entire trip cost spans both brands — this is the highest-value unaddressed persona segment.

**Missed personas:** The cross-brand traveller: someone planning a car trip abroad who needs parking + drop-off + roaming costs in one session. FamilyLinks exists but no journey, no cross-app search, and no combined-cost answer page serves this person — they are forced to context-switch between two separate domains. The repeat/alert subscriber: a user who already booked parking last year and returns specifically to check whether the price has changed or to set a price alert. The email capture, news hub, and freshness tooling are all built for this person but no journey validates that the re-engagement loop closes. The corporate travel manager or expense approver: someone (like Tom's manager reading a screenshot, or Helen's employer reimbursing drop-off) who needs a self-documenting, printable, citable answer — not a personalised calculator. The 'share/print' gap is noted but the persona behind it (the third party who must be convinced, not the traveller themselves) is never named. The assistive-technology-primary user: screen-reader and switch-access users are referenced in accessibility findings but no journey is written from their perspective, so the synthesis cannot rank which a11y gaps cause task failure versus mild friction. Without a named AT persona, the P0 a11y pass has no acceptance criteria. The data journalist or AI crawler: referenced obliquely in direction 2 (GEO/press) but never given a named journey. Without a concrete crawler/journalist scenario the GEO schema recommendations in P4 have no testable success condition — 'citation rate' is asserted as a benchmark but never grounded in a specific query or publication type the site is trying to win.

## Per-page hit-list

### parkmath / (home hub)
- Default AirportSearch to empty or ~6 top-traffic airports; full grid only after typing — recover ~8 viewport-heights and reach StatStrip/tiles without scrolling
- Topic-specific NavTile CTAs ('Compare parking prices →') via optional cta prop; consider promoting Airport-parking tile to position 1 (higher-intent per Priya/James)
- Add aria-live polite region + role=alert to NearbyAirports geolocation status; label the permission prompt with a reason
- Add sr-only section headings (stats/deals/email/family) for a real heading tree; add focus-visible rings to EmailCaptureSlot
- Mobile secondary tiles as a horizontal snap-pill rail; move affiliate disclosure inline (drop the asterisk CTA)

### /drop-off-charges (index)
- Confirm FeeGrid card-row path is live in production (6-col table overflows at 390px) — this is the whole value prop
- SegmentedControl min-h-9 → min-h-11; add Source column / methodology + CSV download + 'Cite this table' (Marcus)
- Add FAQPage + opengraph-image; lead summary with the most-expensive airport; add 'kiss and fly'/'set-down' synonyms to meta + intro
- FreshnessBadge: show oldest-row date or 'X of Y verified in 30 days' instead of newest
- Replace near-duplicate h2 with an additive, action-oriented heading; add an airport quick-jump filter input

### /drop-off-charges/[airport]
- Reorder fold: AnswerCard hero first, then chips/lead; bind calculator result to --text-stat so the card is the one hero number
- Replace hardcoded 'Pay before you leave' chip and 'for up to 1 min' note with real paymentDeadline / per-entry-tariff label
- Hoist freeAlternative Callout + Blue Badge directly under AnswerCard; promote trendNote to a warning Callout with icon + role=note
- Mobile IATA+city pill fallback for hidden UkMap; convert 'More at this airport' to 44px nav-tile cross-links
- Free-airport branch: surface the no-return/10-min gotcha as a warning chip even when isFree; add taxis-pay / grace-period / appeal FAQs

### /airport-parking (index)
- Render savingsVsGatePence as a 'Saves vs gate' column (+£X green) — the strongest pre-book argument, currently computed but dropped
- Add 3/7/14-day duration toggle via ?days= URL param (RSC, no client JS); make rows fully tappable to airport pages
- FreshnessBadge → oldest verifiedAt; embed cheapest price + airport into the meta description
- Add BreadcrumbList JSON-LD; wrap grid in nav aria-label + skip-to-airport-list link
- Gate-only state: explain when no pre-book price exists rather than a misleading single-row 'cheapest' badge

### /airport-parking/[airport] (hub)
- Promote BookingOptions directly below the calculator + 'Book this option' link on the winner row (highest-leverage conversion move)
- Make AnswerLead/SavesVerdict/chart reactive to calculator duration (or scope them '7-day' explicitly) — fixes Priya/Nigel contradiction
- Add gate vs pre-book type badge per calculator row; guard the SVG chart so a £0 / gate-only airport doesn't render a fake comparison
- Token-swap affiliate bg-blue-50 → bg-brand-accent/[0.06]; fix CTA hierarchy (affiliate filled, official-site secondary)
- Wrap chart in figure+figcaption with data-summary alt; duration nav → 44px chips with price sub-labels; add SpeakableSpecification

### /airport-parking/[airport]/[duration]
- Add a display-size hero answer number (--text-display/mf-num) above AnswerLead; pass highlightRow to FeeGrid for the cheapest option
- De-duplicate AnswerLead vs Callout (one carries the answer, the other the 'why it matters' nudge) — halves the fold
- Add aggregateOfferLd/offerLd (builders already exist) — highest-leverage GEO add on this page
- Shorten AnswerLead bullets to one line at 390px (Pre-book/Drive-up labels, drop snapshot dates); CTA copy names airport+duration+price
- Expand buildParkingFaqs to 5–7 duration-specific items; ARIA disclosure pattern for FaqAccordion

### /airport-lounges (index)
- Add datasetLd + breadcrumbLd + FreshnessBadge to match the drop-off index pattern
- Replace the bare lounge-count column with a Priority Pass coverage column; surface Priority Pass in body + AnswerLead
- Add cross-sell to parking/drop-off + a lounge HolidayExtrasCard variant; add a FaqAccordion (What is PP / pre-book vs walk-in / cheapest)
- Rewrite the meta description with airport count, price range, PP coverage, year
- Add aria-label/sr-only 'lounge prices' to airport links; make the mobile card row a tappable unit

### /airport-lounges/[airport]
- Lift SavesVerdict into LoungeCalculator as client output (remove the static 3-visit SSR card that misinforms at other visit counts)
- Add a break-even line ('pays off at N visits/year') as the first result line — the actual answer users came for
- Raise slider max to 52 + add a numeric input; show annual-fee vs per-visit breakdown and a 'card already includes PP' toggle (Tom)
- Pass numericColumns={[1]} so Yes/No renders as left-aligned prose; surface 'PP pre-booking only' as a chip
- Put airport name + verified date inside the result card so screenshots are self-documenting (Tom's expenses)

### /news (hub)
- Surface verifiedAt as a FreshnessBadge pill on cards (highest-trust signal, currently invisible)
- Add RSS autodiscovery link; add category/airport pill filter (typed fields already exist)
- Invert <article>-inside-<a> to the accessible card-link pattern; add sr-only 'increased to' around the before→after arrow
- Add a per-route opengraph-image; add a 3-link conversion row (drop-off/parking/lounges) routed by item.category
- Fix body rendering (prose-sm is a no-op without @tailwindcss/typography) — paragraph array or markdown

### /news/[id]
- Show verifiedAt via FreshnessBadge + conditional 'originally published' line (closes the biggest trust gap)
- sr-only spoken transition for the price-delta arrow; category-aware tinted pill on the change card
- Add a category-adaptive conversion CTA (parking at {airport} / fee-change alerts) — conversion is structurally zero today
- Render supersedes as a banner + isReplacedBy schema; omit the generic OG image rather than emit the wrong one
- Elevate SourceCitation to a 'Source:' pill credential (FT/Reuters pattern)

### /parking-price-index-2026
- Embed the /charts/<slug>.svg images (descriptive alt) + openGraph image — the image-SEO payoff the charts were built for
- Add BreadcrumbList + FAQPage schema; fix SourcesBlock to cite real official airport URLs, not a sibling ParkMath page
- Promote year-on-year movers to a card-strip/table with up/down semantic icons (value is inverted by muted styling)
- Promote CSV links to a 'Download the data' band with typed download buttons (link-magnet conversion anchor)
- Add an on-this-page nav landmark with 44px jump links; make AnswerLead bullets carry orthogonal facts

### /privacy
- Add meta description + canonical + robots (lowest-effort SEO fix on the site)
- Replace the unregistered-company placeholder with a real contact email; remove the registration-status parenthetical
- Update the affiliate disclosure to present tense (Awin is live — ASA/CAP accuracy)
- Use text-ink not text-ink-muted for body (contrast); add micro-headings + a last-updated date stamp
- Confirm the shared header/footer renders correctly at 390px

### roammath / (home)
- Stack StatStrip to 1-col on mobile and stop truncating the verified note; split '£0 (Three)' into hero number + muted context
- 44px touch targets on the CTA link pair (wrap in nav, inline-flex min-h-11)
- Add a destination search/autocomplete above the 40-flag grid; group by region (Taiwan/Nigeria gaps surface here)
- Add SearchAction to webSiteLd for GEO 'lookup tool' classification
- Make FamilyLinks a true card-link (or drop mf-sheen/mf-press); add a static EmailCaptureSlot fallback when the env var is missing

### roammath /roaming (index)
- Add an above-the-fold answer/hero figure (from £2/day Three to £7/day EE) before the flag rail
- Fix the card-row hero so it shows the cheapest network/eSIM, not always EE (first-numeric-column bug)
- Make card rows fully tappable (rowHref) and add a skip-to-table link; group/scroll-cap the 40 flag chips
- Enrich datasetLd (keywords/spatial/temporal/about) for AI citation; add year + price to title/description
- abbr/sr-only for the '†' eSIM footnote; raise its contrast; rewrite the lead answer-first (£0 vs £7/day)

### roammath /roaming/[country]
- Dispatch mf-live-answer events from RoamingCalculator so MiniAnswerBar stops showing the stale SSR string
- Differentiate or suppress the duplicate SavesVerdict vs calculator verdict (don't state the same saving twice)
- Token-swap AffiliateBlock bg-blue-50 → bg-surface/brand tint + 'Recommended option' eyebrow + verify icon
- Surface a multi-source trust line ('Verified against EE/O2/Vodafone & Three') instead of one inline citation
- Network nav → 44px chips; mobile flag+continent chip fallback for hidden RegionMap; suppress filler SavesVerdict when no verdict

### roammath /roaming/[country]/[network]
- Add a duration control (promote RoamingCalculator network-pre-selected, or a no-JS 3/7/14-day tab table) — this page answers a specific intent but can't personalise
- Render SavesVerdict in all three branches; the 'no standard pass' case is the strongest eSIM upsell and renders nothing today
- Split the eSIM bullet out of AnswerLead facts into its own Callout/SavesVerdict; convert the lone back-link to a 44px network nav block
- Add the eSIM price to the 'no standard pass' meta description (the branch most likely to be AI-cited); add explicit robots
- Network+country-specific FAQ h2 and a 3/14-day FAQ item; add a [network]-level opengraph-image

### roammath /baggage-fees (index)
- Add FAQPage + breadcrumbLd (highest-traffic head-query entry point currently has only itemListLd)
- Make mobile card rows and the desktop airline cell tappable links (table is non-interactive today)
- Add a shared dynamic-pricing 'Why a range?' Callout so users don't believe a cabin bag is always free
- Render 'Free' as a teal badge (feeRangeLabel → ReactNode); add a SavesVerdict headline spread; human-format the ISO date
- Rewrite the lead answer-first and device-agnostic ('Tap' not 'Click'); aria-hidden the → glyphs

### roammath /baggage-fees/[airline]
- Cap AnswerLead facts to 2–3 decision-relevant rows (it currently duplicates the entire FeeGrid above it)
- Pass numericColumns={[1]} so the Notes prose column stops rendering as right-aligned tabular figures
- Inject a dynamic-pricing FAQ ('why does the booking price differ?') + itemListLd for fee rows (builder exists)
- Add 'What else costs on your trip?' tiles to /roaming + eSIM (the per-page internal-link/cross-sell opportunity, a lone back-link today)
- Render 'Free' as a positive badge; 44px SourceCitation; ARIA disclosure for FaqAccordion (shared win)

### packages/ui (shared design system)
- Split @theme into brand primitives + semantic aliases now, behind a dark-mode media/[data-theme] block — zero visual change today, no rework later
- Add ~5–8 16px/1.5px stroke icons to line-glyphs (penalty/free-alt/clock/shield-tick/savings) to satisfy 'colour is never the only signal'
- SegmentedControl min-h-9 → min-h-11; @supports-fence the conic mf-edge-shine with a static-hairline fallback for Safari
- Make the FeeGrid winner persistent across breakpoints (mf-glow-winner + left-accent on mobile card and desktop rest state); accept rowHref/highlightRow/highlightColumn
- Add density/cell-spacing tokens; make StatStrip column count dynamic; guard GlintController to no-op when no .mf-glint targets exist
